narrative_summary:
The BRCA2 c.7448G>A (p.Ser2483Asn, S2483N) missense change occurs in the DNA‑damage repair gene BRCA2 and has been evaluated in the context of hereditary breast and ovarian cancer risk.  
In a Brazilian hereditary‑breast‑cancer cohort the variant was detected in a single index case and was reported as a variant of uncertain significance, with no segregation data or definitive pathogenic or benign classification; it was also noted to co‑occur with a pathogenic BRCA1 truncating mutation and to be listed as VUS in multiple databases and by several in‑silico tools [PMC5348334].  
Functional assessment using mouse embryonic stem‑cell–based assays demonstrated that the S2483N protein retained normal activity, and a computational model of these assays classified the variant as functional (benign) with a low allele frequency (0.0038) and a functional (F) prediction score [PMC7722754].  
An independent study that incorporated homology‑directed‑repair functional data into an ACMG/AMP framework re‑evaluated the same variant and assigned it a likely‑benign (LBEN) classification, citing supporting criteria such as BP2, BP4, and BS3 [PMC9433957].  
Collectively, the limited clinical observations label the change as uncertain, but the experimental data consistently indicate normal protein function and the ACMG‑based reanalysis supports a likely‑benign interpretation.  Accordingly, the preponderance of evidence favors classification of BRCA2 S2483N as likely benign, although definitive clinical certainty remains limited by the paucity of segregation and epidemiologic data.

alteration **c.7448G>A** (BRCA2) was identified in a patient carrying the BRCA1 mutation c.1687C>T(p.Gln563Ter). Variants of unknown significance according to 7 databases an 5 in silico prediction programs

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study identifies BRCA2 p.Ser2483Asn (c.7448G>A) in a Brazilian HBOC cohort and classifies it as a variant of uncertain significance with no conclusive pathogenic or benign evidence.
article_mentions.title: Prevalence of BRCA1/BRCA2 mutations in a Brazilian population sample at-risk for hereditary breast cancer and characterization of its genetic ancestry

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article provides functional assay and computational evidence that the BRCA2 S2483N variant behaves as a functional (likely benign) protein in mouse embryonic stem cells, supporting a benign classification.
article_mentions.title: A computational model for classification of BRCA2 variants using mouse embryonic stem cell-based functional assays

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study incorporated HDR functional assay data into ACMG/AMP classification and reclassified BRCA2 S2483N as likely benign (LBEN).',
article_mentions.title: Classification of BRCA2 variants of uncertain significance (VUS) using an ACMG/AMP model incorporating a homology directed repair (HDR) functional assay
